queries.query: Netherlands hyperscale data center construction ban Amsterdam zoning 350 MVA 2030

results.title: Amsterdam | DC Byte
results.text:
Amsterdam | DC Byte

# Amsterdam

Supply: 1,849 MW

Total Supply is the sum of four supply categories: Live, Under Construction, Committed, and Early Stage.

Facilities: 90

## Overview

Amsterdam is one of the most established data centre markets in the world thanks to its geographic position as a gateway to Europe and its world-class internet exchanges, particularly AMS-IX. Major growth was seen between 2010 and 2018 and was then curtailed by a moratorium in 2019. Since the moratorium was lifted in 2020, development has picked up but the rate of activity has reduced with other markets offering strong alternatives.

As with other markets, power availability is a particular challenge for the Amsterdam market, with demand for electricity outstripping supply and leading to ‘traffic jams’ on the power grid. For data centres, power supply is affecting the market with many power distributions firms saying that they have no power available until 2030, thus limiting development.

Significantly, since the start of 2024, an amended decree has meant that no new hyperscale data centres (<10 hectares/70+ MW) are allowed to be constructed in the Netherlands, except for a few locations in the municipalities of Het Hogeland (e.g. Eemshaven) and Hollands Kroon (e.g. Agriport). Further regulation is planned for the Municipality of Amsterdam with a recent draft umbrella zoning plan suggesting that 350 MVA is the new maximum connection capacity within the municipality until 2030. Additionally,

results.title: Challenges in the Dutch Data Center Market | Insights | Greenberg Traurig LLP
results.text:
Challenges in the Dutch Data Center Market | Insights | Greenberg Traurig LLP Skip to main content

Share

The Netherlands, widely acclaimed for its tulips, windmills, and cheese, is also an important player in a different realm: the data center market. Due to its strategic geographical position, its advanced digital infrastructure and its strong commitment to sustainability, the Dutch data center market, particularly in Amsterdam, is significant on the global stage.

Notwithstanding its growth, the Dutch data center market faces various challenges, such as high energy costs, competition for skilled personnel, limited land availability for expansion, power grid congestion and regulatory constraints related to environmental impact. Given rising global demand for data centers, these challenges strain the country’s ability to meet growth expectations essential to keeping the Netherlands competitive in the sector.

One of the current impediments to industry growth is the scarcity of suitable locations in the Netherlands, especially because the Dutch government has imposed new rules for hyperscale data centers, and the Municipality of Amsterdam has issued a preparatory decision restricting the development of new data center projects.

Further to an amendment of the decree concerning rules on the quality of the physical environment and the performance of tasks and authorities (Besluit kwaliteit leefomgeving) that entered into force on 1 January 2024, no hyperscale data centers are

results.title: Increasing control of data centre locations
results.text:
Increasing control of data centre locations

Back

# Increasing control of data centre locations

Article

26.08.2021

NL Law

Expertise

Environment and Planning Public Law Real Estate

Due to the impact of the booming data centre market, Dutch municipalities and provinces adopt data centre policies and umbrella zoning plans to regulate data centre locations. In Amsterdam and Haarlemmermeer in particular, the growth of the data centre ecosystem therefore appears to be coming to a halt.

The data centre market is booming, according to a recent report from global commercial real estate services company JLL. Due to developments including cloud computing, streaming and 5G, there is a great demand for new data centres. In the Metropolitan Area of Amsterdam (Metropool Regio Amsterdam, MRA), a large cluster of data centres has arisen due to the favourable geographical location of the Netherlands within Europe, the large number of available sea cables, and the Amsterdam Internet Exchange (AMS-IX), one of the largest internet hubs in the world. In addition, the high availability of sustainable energy contributes to the establishment of hyperscale data centres in, for instance, the Eemshaven area in the north part of the Netherlands, and in Middenmeer, to the north of Amsterdam.

However, the increasing number of data centres also has an impact on the landscape and on energy infrastructure. For this reason, the municipality of Amsterdam has adopted a data centre policy, the municipali

results.title: Dutch Data Center Association says new Amsterdam rules are "symbol politics" - DCD
results.text:
Dutch Data Center Association says new Amsterdam rules are "symbol politics" - DCD

07 May 2026

# NorthC data center outside Amsterdam suffers fire

# The Neocloud Supplement

07 May 2026

9–11 June 2026

# Connect DCD>Connect | APAC 2026

# Dutch Data Center Association says new Amsterdam rules are "symbol politics"

The Municipality is restricting a vital sector - which already can't get the power it needs, says DDA

January 27, 2024 By Peter Judge Have your say

The Dutch Data Center Association (DDA) has complained about "drastic" new measures from the Municipality of Amsterdam to restrict data centers.

The new rules, announced in a "preparatory decision" in late 2023, will limit data centers in the Amsterdam municipality (the red area of the map), which is part of the larger Metropole Region.

– Dutch Datacenter Association

From now on, "new data centers may only be built on business parks in Amsterdam and Haarlemmermeer, both in the Amsterdam Metropolitan Area (MRA) and in Hollands Kroon," according to the municipality.

The rules follow a long period of discussion between the data center sector and the Amsterdam authorities which imposed a one-year moratorium on new data center building which was announced suddenly in 2019. Construction has been allowed again since 2020.

In 2022, the city authorities passed a law that could impose fines on colocation providers who do not put idle servers into sleep mode. DCD understands the law still stands, though it has not been

results.title: Clear agreements and robust measures prevent a widespread grid connection pause in the Flevopolder, Gelderland and Utrecht – connection pause does apply to a sub‑area
results.text:
Clear agreements and robust measures prevent a widespread grid connection pause in the Flevopolder, Gelderland and Utrecht – connection pause does apply to a sub‑area

Skip to content

# Clear agreements and robust measures prevent a widespread grid connection pause in the Flevopolder, Gelderland and Utrecht – connection pause does apply to a sub‑area

Today, the State Secretary for Climate and Green Growth (KGG), Jo‑Annes de Bat, informed the House of Representatives by letter about the progress of the approach to the overloaded electricity grid in the Flevopolder, Gelderland and Utrecht (FGU). Following intensive cooperation in recent months between the Ministry of EZK and, among others, the ACM, grid operators TenneT, Stedin and Liander, and the provinces of Flevoland, Gelderland and Utrecht, a general connection pause in the region has been averted. A connection pause does apply as of now in the sub‑area Breukelen‑Kortrijk.

Published22/04/2026

Last updated23/04/2026

In February, TenneT warned of an impending connection stop in the FGU area and called for joint, robust interventions to prevent large‑scale power outages. Only in this way can we continue to carry out our statutory task of safeguarding the safety and reliability of the high‑voltage grid.

# Update Netcapaciteit

Dit is de terugkerende update over de capaciteit van het elektriciteitsnet van Stedin. Deze update is voor klanten met een grootverbruik aansluiting en voor gemeenten, provincies en stakeholders in ons verzorgingsgebied met o.a. Utrecht, Zuid-Holland, Zeeland en de Rotterdamse haven.

### Aankondiging congestie station Hellevoetsluis 25/10 kV

Op 1 juli 2026 kondigt Stedin congestie voor afname (verbruik) aan voor station Hellevoetsluis 25/10 kV en start hier een onderzoek naar congestiemanagement. De congestie treft zowel grootverbruik- als kleinverbruikklanten in Hellevoetsluis, Westvoorne-Zuid en buitengebieden. results.title: Ruimte op het Zeeuwse stroomnet voor 61 bedrijven | Stedin
results.text:
Ruimte op het Zeeuwse stroomnet voor 61 bedrijven | Stedin

❮ Terug naar Persberichten

Rotterdam, 19 juni 2025

Netbeheerders Stedin en TenneT kunnen naar verwachting 61 Zeeuwse bedrijven en instellingen de gevraagde ruimte geven op het stroomnet voor een nieuwe of zwaardere aansluiting. Voor het eerst sinds het Zeeuwse hoogspanningsnet in de zomer van 2023 zijn maximale capaciteit bereikte, gaat een eerste groep klanten van de wachtlijst af. Dit gaat om bedrijven en instellingen verdeeld over de hele provincie Zeeland. De ruimte komt vrij doordat TenneT eerder dit jaar een contract sloot voor flexibel vermogen met Lion Storage, onderdeel van Return, voor een grote batterij-installatie in de haven van Vlissingen. Ondernemers en instellingen voor wie dit geldt worden hier deze week over gebeld door hun netbeheerder. Een deel van de klanten die ruimte op het stroomnet krijgt, zijn instellingen die op basis van maatschappelijk prioriteren als eerste in aanmerking komen. Maatschappelijk prioriteren wil zeggen dat maatschappelijk belangrijke projecten zoals scholen en ziekenhuizen met voorrang worden toegelaten op het stroomnet volgens de regels die de ACM hiervoor heeft opgesteld. Alle Zeeuwse klanten met voorrang krijgen de stroomcapaciteit die ze hebben aangevraagd.

#### Wanneer ruimte op stroomnet

Bij de bekendmaking van het flexcontract met Lion Storage in maart van dit jaar, is bedrijven op de wachtlijst opnieuw de mogelijkheid geboden om prioriteit aan te vragen. Daarna

results.title: Thousands of Dutch face up to three years’ delays for higher-capacity grid connections | NL Times
results.text:
Thousands of Dutch face up to three years’ delays for higher-capacity grid connections | NL Times

Electrical grids. - Credit: brianguest / DepositPhotos- License: DepositPhotos

» More tags « Less tags

Tuesday, 2 June 2026 - 22:00

## Share this article:

# Thousands of Dutch face up to three years’ delays for higher-capacity grid connections

A sharp rise in requests for higher-capacity electricity connections and a reported grid “closure” in parts of Utrecht are straining the Dutch power network, with about 7,300 households now on waiting lists and some facing delays of up to three years for upgrades, De Telegraaf reports.

Grid operator Liander reported 27,500 household applications for higher-capacity grid connections in the first five months of 2026, up more than 50 percent from 18,000 in the same period a year earlier. Requests for new household connections rose 30 percent.

At the same time, Stedin said parts of Utrecht’s electricity network were effectively “closed" for new capacity for an indefinite period, following the introduction of tighter limits on available grid space.

Liander has now placed roughly 7,300 households on a waiting list for the first time. Some applicants may wait up to three years before receiving a new or upgraded connection, according to reports. The increase is largely driven by households installing heat pumps, electric vehicle charging stations, and other electrified home systems that require more grid capacity.

results.text:
Solar & Storage Magazine - Stroomnet Stedin in Barendrecht en Ridderkerk vol tot 2033

Nieuws

© Stedin

3 juni 2026

# Stroomnet Stedin in Barendrecht en Ridderkerk vol tot 2033

## Netbeheerder Stedin heeft een nieuwe congestiemelding gedaan voor Barendrecht en Ridderkerk. Grootverbruikers in de regio komen op een wachtlijst tot uiterlijk 2033 als de uitbreiding van het stroomnet klaar is.

De nieuwe melding raakt de oude kern van Barendrecht, de west- en zuidkant van Ridderkerk en enkele buitengebieden die deels in de gemeenten Rotterdam, Zwijndrecht en Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht liggen.

Gestapelde congestie Bijzonder aan deze situatie is dat het gaat om zogeheten gestapelde congestie: het stroomnet is op meerdere niveaus tegelijk vol. Zowel het hoogspanningsnet van TenneT, het 150-kilovoltstation Langeland van Stedin als het onderliggende 50/13-kilovoltstation Ridderkerk hebben de maximale capaciteit bereikt.

In de regio was al eerder congestie afgekondigd in Rotterdam-Zuid door Stedin voor station Langeland en door TenneT voor het bovenliggende landelijke net in Noordelijk Zuid-Holland. Aanvragen van grootverbruikers staan sindsdien al op de wachtlijst. De nieuwe melding verandert daar niets aan; die bestaande wachtlijstposities blijven ongewijzigd.

Gevolgen nieuwe aanvragen Bedrijven en instellingen die vanaf 2 juni een nieuwe aanvraag indienen of extra transportvermogen aanvragen voor afname van elektriciteit, komen ook op de wachtlijst voor station Ridderkerk. Transportve

results.title: State of the Dutch Data Centers 2025 - Dutch Data Center Association
results.text:
State of the Dutch Data Centers 2025 - Dutch Data Center Association

## Publication

# State of the Dutch Data Centers 2025

With pride, we present the anniversary edition of our annual State of the Dutch Data Centers report, this year themed: “From Boom to Bottlenecks.” This special edition marks ten years of independent data center analysis and highlights the sector’s evolution into an essential pillar of the Dutch digital economy.

The annual DDA market report is compiled based on research among members of the Dutch Data Center Association, in collaboration with Pb7 Research. The report offers a unique overview of the current state of the sector and delves into trends, investments, sustainability, regional growth, and the impact of emerging technologies such as AI.

From Boom to Bottlenecks The theme “From Boom to Bottlenecks” not only reflects the successes of the past decade, but also underscores the need for shared responsibility for the future. Data centers are no longer a niche phenomenon, they are fundamental to innovation, economic resilience, and digital sovereignty.

results.title: Ten Years of State of the Dutch Data Centers: A Decade of Growth and Challenges - Dutch Data Center Association
results.text:
Ten Years of State of the Dutch Data Centers: A Decade of Growth and Challenges - Dutch Data Center Association

## News

# Ten Years of State of the Dutch Data Centers: A Decade of Growth and Challenges

Today, the Dutch Data Center Association (DDA) presents the anniversary edition of the State of the Dutch Data Centers report during National Data Center Day. The report offers a unique overview of ten years of data center development in the Netherlands and shows how the sector has evolved into a cornerstone of our digital economy.

Market Outlook 2025: AI and Scale-up Drive Market Dynamics

The Dutch data center market continues to grow despite ongoing challenges related to grid congestion and permitting procedures. In 2024, the total colocation capacity reached 924 megawatts. For 2025, the sector expects a record investment of more than €1.4 billion. Increasingly, data center operators are building scalable colocation campuses to meet the rapidly growing demand for computing power driven by AI and cloud applications. At the same time, enterprise data centers are losing ground, as organizations increasingly opt for colocation or cloud infrastructure.

Regional Expansion: Groningen, South Holland, and North Brabant

While the Amsterdam Metropolitan Area (MRA) still accounts for approximately 70% of colocation capacity, growth is shifting toward regions such as Groningen, South Holland, and North Brabant. These regions often offer greater grid capacity, less complex permittin

## Dutch data centre association expects record EUR 1.4 bln investment in 2025

NETHERLANDS13 JUN 2025 The data centre market is entering a new growth phase in the Netherlands, driven by demand for AI infrastructure and scalable colocation capacity. Almost EUR 1.4 billion should be invested in the construction and equipment of colocation data centres this year, a sharp increase from previous years, according to an annual report from the Dutch Datacenter Association (DDA).

results.title: Tien jaar State of the Dutch Data Centers: een decennium van bloei en blokkades - Dutch Data Center Association
results.text:
Tien jaar State of the Dutch Data Centers: een decennium van bloei en blokkades - Dutch Data Center Association

## Nieuws

# Tien jaar State of the Dutch Data Centers: een decennium van bloei en blokkades

Vandaag presenteert de Dutch Data Center Association (DDA) de jubileumeditie van het rapport State of the Dutch Data Centers tijdens de Nationale Datacenter Dag. Het rapport biedt een uniek overzicht van tien jaar datacenterontwikkeling in Nederland en laat zien hoe de sector zich heeft ontwikkeld tot fundament van de digitale economie.

Marktanalyse 2025: AI en schaalvergroting zetten markt op scherp

De Nederlandse datacentermarkt blijft groeien, ondanks aanhoudende uitdagingen rondom netcongestie en vergunningstrajecten. In 2024 bereikte het totale colocatievermogen 924 megawatt. Voor 2025 verwacht de sector een recordinvestering van meer dan €1,4 miljard. Steeds vaker bouwen datacenterexploitanten schaalbare colocatiecampussen die inspelen op de snel groeiende vraag naar rekenkracht voor AI- en cloudtoepassingen. Tegelijkertijd verliezen enterprise datacenters terrein; organisaties kiezen steeds vaker voor colocatie of cloud infrastructuur.

Regionale groei: Groningen, Zuid-Holland en Noord-Brabant

Hoewel de Metropoolregio Amsterdam (MRA) nog altijd goed is voor circa 70% van de colocatiecapaciteit, verschuift de groei richting regio’s zoals Groningen, Zuid-Holland en Noord-Brabant. Daar is vaak meer netcapaciteit beschikbaar, liggen vergunningstrajecten minder comple

results.title: State of the Dutch Data Centers 2026 - Dutch Data Center Association
results.text:
State of the Dutch Data Centers 2026 - Dutch Data Center Association

## Publication

# State of the Dutch Data Centers 2026

Hierbij presenteren wij de 2026 editie van ons ‘State of the Dutch Data Centers’ rapport.

Stroomtekort wordt groeitekort: Nederlandse datacenters bieden energiesysteem een uitweg uit netcongestie

De Nederlandse datacentermarkt staat op een kantelpunt. De vraag naar AI-rekenkracht groeit snel, maar stroomvoorziening, netcongestie en vergunningen bepalen steeds vaker de grenzen van groei. Dat blijkt uit State of the Dutch Data Centers 2026 van de DDA.

Waar datacenters vaak als oorzaak van netcongestie worden gezien, laat het rapport zien dat ze via flexibiliteit, opslag, workload-sturing en restwarmte juist kunnen bijdragen aan een stabieler energiesysteem.

Tegelijk neemt de druk toe: Europa wil datacentercapaciteit verdrievoudigen voor digitale soevereiniteit, terwijl Nederland wordt geremd door lange vergunningstrajecten en oplopende wachttijden voor netaansluitingen.
